reasoning: The March 2025 election in Greenland was won by the Demokraatit party, which explicitly campaigned on a 'slow approach' to independence from Denmark. A broad coalition government (holding 23 of 31 seats) was formed on March 28, 2025, led by Demokraatit's Jens-Frederik Nielsen. This coalition excluded the staunchly pro-independence Naleraq party. On April 1, 2025, the new Prime Minister emphasized building a 'strong partnership with Denmark... until the day we can be sovereign'. Given the clear 'gradualist' mandate of the new government—which was largely formed to show unity and maintain ties with Denmark in response to U.S. annexation rhetoric—and the extremely short timeline (<2 months) remaining before July 1, 2025, there is virtually no chance of an independence referendum date being officially announced by then.
